title: Enterprise Patterns for ML Systems
description: Applying Fowler's Patterns of Enterprise Application Architecture to 3DGS inference pipelines and ML research systems.
learningObjectives: Explain the three-layer architecture and apply the dependency rule to decompose a 3DGS inference service into Presentation, Domain, and Data Source layers.
Select the appropriate domain logic pattern — Transaction Script, Domain Model, Table Module, or Service Layer — given a description of system complexity and team constraints.
Implement the Data Mapper + Unit of Work + Identity Map triad to achieve complete isolation between a Gaussian splatting domain model and its persistence layer.
Diagnose the N+1 query problem in both database contexts and ML batch inference pipelines, and apply Lazy Load or eager fetching to resolve it.
Distinguish Optimistic Offline Lock from Pessimistic Offline Lock and justify which to use in distributed 3DGS training based on conflict frequency and throughput requirements.
Design a coarse-grained Remote Facade and corresponding Data Transfer Objects for a 3DGS compression service, quantifying the latency savings over fine-grained remote calls.
